segments.text: Israel is an empty vine, he bringeth forth fruit unto himself:
according to the multitude of his fruit he hath increased the altars;
according to the goodness of his land they have made goodly images.

footnotes: an empty vine: or, a vine emptying the fruit which it giveth
images: Heb. statues, or, standing images

segments.text: Their heart is divided;
now shall they be found faulty:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: he shall break down their altars, he shall spoil their images.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: Their heart is divided: or, He hath divided their heart
images: Heb. statues, or, standing images

segments.text: We have no king, because we feared not the LORD; what then should a king do to us?
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: They have spoken words, swearing falsely in making a covenant:
thus judgment springeth up as hemlock in the furrows of the field.
The inhabitants of Samaria shall fear because of the calves of Beth-aven:
for the people thereof shall mourn over it, and the priests thereof that rejoiced on it, for the glory thereof, because it is departed from it.
It shall be also carried unto Assyria for a present to king Jareb:
Ephraim shall receive shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: As for Samaria, her king is cut off as the foam upon the water.

footnotes: the water: Heb. the face of the water

segments.text: The high places also of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the thorn and the thistle shall come up on their altars;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Cover us; and to the hills, Fall on us.
segments.speaker: People of Israel

segments.text: O Israel, thou hast sinned from the days of Gibeah: there they stood: the battle in Gibeah against the children of iniquity did not overtake them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: It is in my desire that I should chastise them;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: and the people shall be gathered against them, when they shall bind themselves in their two furrows.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: when they shall bind themselves: or, when I shall bind them for their two transgressions, or, in their two habitations

segments.text: And Ephraim is as an heifer that is taught, and loveth to tread out the corn;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: but I passed over upon her fair neck: I will make Ephraim to ride; Judah shall plow, and Jacob shall break his clods.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: her fair neck: Heb. the beauty of her neck

segments.text: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap in mercy; break up your fallow ground: for it is time to seek the LORD, till he come and rain righteousness upon you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Ye have plowed wickedness, ye have reaped iniquity; ye have eaten the fruit of lies: because thou didst trust in thy way, in the multitude of thy mighty men.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people, and all thy fortresses shall be spoiled, as Shalman spoiled Beth-arbel in the day of battle: the mother was dashed in pieces upon her children.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So shall Beth-el do unto you because of your great wickedness: in a morning shall the king of Israel utterly be cut off.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: your great wickedness: Heb. the evil of your evil
